  • candidate 68dd2a92 — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Prior mid-leap attempts always confounded asymmetric sweep with a material swap, a wedge change, or under/over-grown plates and all failed outright; this pivot isolates the frog-leap trait cleanly by holding the elite's proven carbon truss arm, maxed wedge, and elite deck_gap fixed while braced front arms sit near stock (30deg) and rear arms sweep far past stock (53deg) with plate_length/width_sca
  • candidate 7ee157de — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Webbed-foot arms have only ever been tested paired with a maxed wedge or with asymmetric sweep (both landed worse than elite); this pivot instead pairs a wide, barely-waisted, membrane-thin carbon arm (width 1.1, waist 1.1, thickness 0.0045) with the resting-frog tuck -- short arm_length_scale (0.80) and near-flat battery wedge (1.0deg) -- on stock sweep and unit plates, testing whether area-sprea
  • candidate 1d0f358f — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Tucked-frog mass cut: shrink wheelbase into the untested safe band between the failed 0.78 (rotor overlap) and the elite floor of ~0.935, keeping every other lever at its proven-safe elite value so only the arm-length/disk-loading tradeoff is isolated.

✦ claude designer · generation 23 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182, g18 8.161, g19 8.161, g20 8.126, g21 8.126, g22 8.126 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 10 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in
- g18 `364861df` fitness 8.621 Wh/km-agg — was: The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather than
- g18 `44d60067` fitness 9.071 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting fla
- g18 `677ff932` fitness 9.350 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty once
- g19 `930f0f43` fitness 9.544 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is carbon at arm-mass floor; PLA+ has never been properly tested (its one random trial failed from arm overstress, not tip deflection), so this design breaks the monoculture with PLA+ on stock-adjacent, low-risk geometry but gives the arm real cross-section (width 1.0, light waist 0.85, thickness 0.007) and plates at 0.85x (above the 0.8x printed-material floor) to finally survive wher
- g19 `99aabf80` fitness 8.671 Wh/km-agg — was: Past probes changed only one lever at a time (wheelbase alone, sweep alone, wedge alone, gap alone) and each underperformed in isolation; this design stacks all four moderately at once: a modest 1.08 wheelbase stretch, real-but-restrained sweep triangulation (37/42) with matching 1.05 plate growth, generous 0.037 deck_gap to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode, and a still-strong
- g19 `cc389578` fitness 8.657 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite shares one trait: frontal area cut via maxed battery wedge on near-stock geometry, so this pivot abandons the wedge entirely (2deg) and instead braces the airframe with genuine sweep triangulation (40/45 vs stock 31.4/36) plus a stretched 1.15 wheelbase for lower disk loading, growing plates 1.10/1.10 to keep tongue bolts anchored and testing whether geometric bracing plus moment-arm r
- g20 `13a83031` fitness 9.313 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetry: soften the failed 30.5/55 deg sweep split to 32/48 deg and grow plate_length/width_scale past the rule-of-thumb minimum (1.15/1.18 vs the ~1.12 needed) so the long swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored, reinvesting the abandoned battery-wedge budget into rear arm reinforcement instead.
- g20 `5e3f8986` fitness 10.562 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the all-carbon floor-mass tension-truss for frog-skin compliance: PETG with a wide, only lightly waisted arm and real thickness margin (well past the thickness/waist combo that killed every prior printed-material probe), on safe stock-adjacent geometry so only the material+cross-section axis changes.
- g21 `4c7dffe1` fitness 9.633 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite converges on a floor-mass, deep-waisted (0.55) tension-truss arm that has plateaued at ~8.1 while storm and cold_headwind remain the dominant costs -- webbed-foot logic replaces the narrow thick truss with a wide, barely-waisted, thin flange (width 1.15, waist 1.15, thickness 0.0045) that spreads load over area at similar mass, trading maxed battery wedge (14.5->8) and thin decks (0.72
- g21 `f4c0a73c` fitness 9.932 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetric sweep (rear 55deg vs 36 stock) paired with the proven PA12-CF reinforced arm cross-section from a prior successful probe (width 0.95, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065), but this time growing plate_length/width_scale to 1.42/1.32 -- well past the ~1.38 the tongue-bolt rule of thumb demands -- so the swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored where the prior attempt's tighter 1.28/1.
- g22 `5fd2ba8e` fitness 9.793 Wh/km-agg — was: Webbed-feet logic isolated as a single clean swap against the elite baseline: keep every proven elite lever (stock-adjacent sweep, unit plate scales, maxed battery wedge, floor-thin carbon) unchanged and only replace the deep-waisted narrow truss arm with a near-maximum-width, minimally-waisted membrane-thin flange that spreads load over area instead of concentrating it in a truss.
- g22 `c9158c37` fitness 9.984 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetry done safely: short braced front arms at the sweep floor paired with long swept rear arms (+12deg over stock) on the already-validated webbed-foot carbon cross-section (width 1.1/waist 1.1/thickness 0.0045), growing plate scales well past the tongue-bolt rule of thumb (1.20/1.18) and reinvesting the abandoned wedge budget into deck_gap margin.

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Frogs

Think of the shapes of frogs when coming up with new ideas.

- A frog at rest is a low, wide squat: mass carried close to the ground
  plane, limbs folded tight against the body. What is the quadcopter
  equivalent of tucking in — minimal deck gap, battery laid flat, arms
  hugging the plate?
- A frog mid-leap is the opposite: limbs at full asymmetric extension,
  rear legs long and powerful, front legs short and braced for landing.
  Try strongly asymmetric sweep — long swept-back rear arms, short
  forward arms.
- Webbed feet spread load over area with almost no mass. Where can a
  wide, thin structure replace a thick one?
- Frog skin is compliant; frogs absorb landings through flex, not
  stiffness. Consider the flexible filaments with generous arm width
  instead of maximum-stiffness carbon.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 10 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 79e8d5ee — Every printed-material and asymmetric-sweep probe so far failed alone (arm deflection or under-grown plates); this pivot commits to frog mid-leap fully -- short braced front arms near stock, rear arms swept far past stock (54 deg) on a wide, lightly-waisted PLA+ arm for skin-like compliance instead of carbon stiffness, with plate scales grown well past the tongue-bolt rule of thumb and printed-matflew at 10.643 Wh/km
  • candidate c213bc81 — Prior webbed-foot swaps always kept the elite's maxed wedge and thin decks, so they never isolated whether area-spread load-bearing itself helps; this pivot abandons the wedge entirely, keeps stock-adjacent sweep, and reinvests the freed budget into a genuinely wide near-max-width, minimally-waisted membrane arm plus generous deck_gap and thicker decks, directly targeting the storm scenario's cleaflew at 9.785 Wh/km
  • candidate a4be9cf2 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — The entire elite cluster is built on one trait -- floor-mass deep-waisted truss arms plus a maxed battery wedge for frontal area -- and storm/cold_headwind costs have barely moved in 10 gens, so this pivot abandons the wedge trick entirely for genuine frog-at-rest tucking: near-min wheelbase, flat battery (no wedge), and deck_gap trimmed to just above the FC-stack floor, testing whether a low tuck
  • candidate 6e866596 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Tucked-frog squat that abandons the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick for geometric compactness (flat battery, minimal deck gap, short wheelbase), fixing the prior 0.78 arm_length_scale that caused rotor-disk overlap by growing it to 0.90 -- still shorter than every current elite's 0.935-0.965 -- while leaving stock-adjacent sweep and the proven floor-mass carbon truss untouched so only the compactne

✦ claude designer · generation 22 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182, g18 8.161, g19 8.161, g20 8.126, g21 8.126 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 9 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in
- g18 `364861df` fitness 8.621 Wh/km-agg — was: The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather than
- g18 `44d60067` fitness 9.071 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting fla
- g18 `677ff932` fitness 9.350 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty once
- g19 `930f0f43` fitness 9.544 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is carbon at arm-mass floor; PLA+ has never been properly tested (its one random trial failed from arm overstress, not tip deflection), so this design breaks the monoculture with PLA+ on stock-adjacent, low-risk geometry but gives the arm real cross-section (width 1.0, light waist 0.85, thickness 0.007) and plates at 0.85x (above the 0.8x printed-material floor) to finally survive wher
- g19 `99aabf80` fitness 8.671 Wh/km-agg — was: Past probes changed only one lever at a time (wheelbase alone, sweep alone, wedge alone, gap alone) and each underperformed in isolation; this design stacks all four moderately at once: a modest 1.08 wheelbase stretch, real-but-restrained sweep triangulation (37/42) with matching 1.05 plate growth, generous 0.037 deck_gap to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode, and a still-strong
- g19 `cc389578` fitness 8.657 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite shares one trait: frontal area cut via maxed battery wedge on near-stock geometry, so this pivot abandons the wedge entirely (2deg) and instead braces the airframe with genuine sweep triangulation (40/45 vs stock 31.4/36) plus a stretched 1.15 wheelbase for lower disk loading, growing plates 1.10/1.10 to keep tongue bolts anchored and testing whether geometric bracing plus moment-arm r
- g20 `13a83031` fitness 9.313 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetry: soften the failed 30.5/55 deg sweep split to 32/48 deg and grow plate_length/width_scale past the rule-of-thumb minimum (1.15/1.18 vs the ~1.12 needed) so the long swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored, reinvesting the abandoned battery-wedge budget into rear arm reinforcement instead.
- g20 `5e3f8986` fitness 10.562 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the all-carbon floor-mass tension-truss for frog-skin compliance: PETG with a wide, only lightly waisted arm and real thickness margin (well past the thickness/waist combo that killed every prior printed-material probe), on safe stock-adjacent geometry so only the material+cross-section axis changes.

- g21 `f4c0a73c` fitness 9.932 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetric sweep (rear 55deg vs 36 stock) paired with the proven PA12-CF reinforced arm cross-section from a prior successful probe (width 0.95, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065), but this time growing plate_length/width_scale to 1.42/1.32 -- well past the ~1.38 the tongue-bolt rule of thumb demands -- so the swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored where the prior attempt's tighter 1.28/1.

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Frogs

Think of the shapes of frogs when coming up with new ideas.

- A frog at rest is a low, wide squat: mass carried close to the ground
  plane, limbs folded tight against the body. What is the quadcopter
  equivalent of tucking in — minimal deck gap, battery laid flat, arms
  hugging the plate?
- A frog mid-leap is the opposite: limbs at full asymmetric extension,
  rear legs long and powerful, front legs short and braced for landing.
  Try strongly asymmetric sweep — long swept-back rear arms, short
  forward arms.
- Webbed feet spread load over area with almost no mass. Where can a
  wide, thin structure replace a thick one?
- Frog skin is compliant; frogs absorb landings through flex, not
  stiffness. Consider the flexible filaments with generous arm width
  instead of maximum-stiffness carbon.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 9 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 5fd2ba8e — Webbed-feet logic isolated as a single clean swap against the elite baseline: keep every proven elite lever (stock-adjacent sweep, unit plate scales, maxed battery wedge, floor-thin carbon) unchanged and only replace the deep-waisted narrow truss arm with a near-maximum-width, minimally-waisted membrane-thin flange that spreads load over area instead of concentrating it in a truss.flew at 9.793 Wh/km
  • candidate c9158c37 — Frog mid-leap asymmetry done safely: short braced front arms at the sweep floor paired with long swept rear arms (+12deg over stock) on the already-validated webbed-foot carbon cross-section (width 1.1/waist 1.1/thickness 0.0045), growing plate scales well past the tongue-bolt rule of thumb (1.20/1.18) and reinvesting the abandoned wedge budget into deck_gap margin.flew at 9.984 Wh/km
  • candidate ddc60283 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every elite shares a minimum-mass carbon tension-truss arm plus a maxed battery wedge to cut frontal area -- this proposal abandons both at once for a frog-at-rest tuck: minimal deck_gap, a nearly flat battery, and a wide, barely-waisted arm on a compliant PLA+ that absorbs storm/gust loads through flex rather than stiffness.
  • candidate 826db61c — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Frog mid-leap asymmetry taken to its structural limit: a short, near-stock braced front leg paired with a rear sweep pushed to the plate-scale ceiling (56deg, plate scales grown to 1.28/1.22 per the tongue-bolt rule) while keeping the proven floor-mass carbon truss and trading the maxed battery wedge for reinvested rear-arm bracing budget, isolating pure asymmetric-sweep bracing from every prior p
  • candidate ce769011 — rejected pre-flight (rotor too close to deck/battery) — Frog-at-rest tuck: abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and thin-deck floor entirely, using the already-validated PLA+ reinforced arm cross-section (width 1.05/waist 0.85/thickness 0.0065) with a flat battery and tight deck_gap, keeping arm_length at a proven-safe 0.95 and stock-adjacent sweep so no tip-clearance or bolt-miss risk is introduced.

✦ claude designer · generation 21 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182, g18 8.161, g19 8.161, g20 8.126 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 8 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in
- g18 `364861df` fitness 8.621 Wh/km-agg — was: The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather than
- g18 `44d60067` fitness 9.071 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting fla
- g18 `677ff932` fitness 9.350 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty once
- g19 `930f0f43` fitness 9.544 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is carbon at arm-mass floor; PLA+ has never been properly tested (its one random trial failed from arm overstress, not tip deflection), so this design breaks the monoculture with PLA+ on stock-adjacent, low-risk geometry but gives the arm real cross-section (width 1.0, light waist 0.85, thickness 0.007) and plates at 0.85x (above the 0.8x printed-material floor) to finally survive wher
- g19 `99aabf80` fitness 8.671 Wh/km-agg — was: Past probes changed only one lever at a time (wheelbase alone, sweep alone, wedge alone, gap alone) and each underperformed in isolation; this design stacks all four moderately at once: a modest 1.08 wheelbase stretch, real-but-restrained sweep triangulation (37/42) with matching 1.05 plate growth, generous 0.037 deck_gap to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode, and a still-strong
- g19 `cc389578` fitness 8.657 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite shares one trait: frontal area cut via maxed battery wedge on near-stock geometry, so this pivot abandons the wedge entirely (2deg) and instead braces the airframe with genuine sweep triangulation (40/45 vs stock 31.4/36) plus a stretched 1.15 wheelbase for lower disk loading, growing plates 1.10/1.10 to keep tongue bolts anchored and testing whether geometric bracing plus moment-arm r
- g20 `13a83031` fitness 9.313 Wh/km-agg — was: Frog mid-leap asymmetry: soften the failed 30.5/55 deg sweep split to 32/48 deg and grow plate_length/width_scale past the rule-of-thumb minimum (1.15/1.18 vs the ~1.12 needed) so the long swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored, reinvesting the abandoned battery-wedge budget into rear arm reinforcement instead.
- g20 `5e3f8986` fitness 10.562 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the all-carbon floor-mass tension-truss for frog-skin compliance: PETG with a wide, only lightly waisted arm and real thickness margin (well past the thickness/waist combo that killed every prior printed-material probe), on safe stock-adjacent geometry so only the material+cross-section axis changes.

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Frogs

Think of the shapes of frogs when coming up with new ideas.

- A frog at rest is a low, wide squat: mass carried close to the ground
  plane, limbs folded tight against the body. What is the quadcopter
  equivalent of tucking in — minimal deck gap, battery laid flat, arms
  hugging the plate?
- A frog mid-leap is the opposite: limbs at full asymmetric extension,
  rear legs long and powerful, front legs short and braced for landing.
  Try strongly asymmetric sweep — long swept-back rear arms, short
  forward arms.
- Webbed feet spread load over area with almost no mass. Where can a
  wide, thin structure replace a thick one?
- Frog skin is compliant; frogs absorb landings through flex, not
  stiffness. Consider the flexible filaments with generous arm width
  instead of maximum-stiffness carbon.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 8 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 4c7dffe1 — Every elite converges on a floor-mass, deep-waisted (0.55) tension-truss arm that has plateaued at ~8.1 while storm and cold_headwind remain the dominant costs -- webbed-foot logic replaces the narrow thick truss with a wide, barely-waisted, thin flange (width 1.15, waist 1.15, thickness 0.0045) that spreads load over area at similar mass, trading maxed battery wedge (14.5->8) and thin decks (0.72flew at 9.633 Wh/km
  • candidate f4c0a73c — Frog mid-leap asymmetric sweep (rear 55deg vs 36 stock) paired with the proven PA12-CF reinforced arm cross-section from a prior successful probe (width 0.95, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065), but this time growing plate_length/width_scale to 1.42/1.32 -- well past the ~1.38 the tongue-bolt rule of thumb demands -- so the swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored where the prior attempt's tighter 1.28/1.flew at 9.932 Wh/km

  • candidate c9319f46 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge (14.5deg) atop a minimum-mass frame -- this design tests the frog-at-rest squat instead: flatten the battery entirely (wedge=0), fold the wheelbase in tight (arm_length_scale 0.85) and near-minimum deck_gap (0.025), then reinvest all the freed mass budget into real structural armor (arm width 1.0/waist 0.8/thickness 0.0065,
  • candidate 52910a5f — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Frog-at-rest squat: pull deck_gap down to 0.026 and flatten the battery wedge to 0 for a low tucked profile, replace the elite family's deep carbon truss with a wide (1.05) unwaisted webbed-foot PETG flange for load-spreading compliance instead of stiffness, and restore arm_length_scale to 0.93 (from the 0.85 that caused rotor overlap) to keep tip clearance safe.

✦ claude designer · generation 20 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182, g18 8.161, g19 8.161 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 7 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in
- g18 `364861df` fitness 8.621 Wh/km-agg — was: The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather than
- g18 `44d60067` fitness 9.071 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting fla
- g18 `677ff932` fitness 9.350 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty once
- g19 `930f0f43` fitness 9.544 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is carbon at arm-mass floor; PLA+ has never been properly tested (its one random trial failed from arm overstress, not tip deflection), so this design breaks the monoculture with PLA+ on stock-adjacent, low-risk geometry but gives the arm real cross-section (width 1.0, light waist 0.85, thickness 0.007) and plates at 0.85x (above the 0.8x printed-material floor) to finally survive wher
- g19 `99aabf80` fitness 8.671 Wh/km-agg — was: Past probes changed only one lever at a time (wheelbase alone, sweep alone, wedge alone, gap alone) and each underperformed in isolation; this design stacks all four moderately at once: a modest 1.08 wheelbase stretch, real-but-restrained sweep triangulation (37/42) with matching 1.05 plate growth, generous 0.037 deck_gap to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode, and a still-strong
- g19 `cc389578` fitness 8.657 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite shares one trait: frontal area cut via maxed battery wedge on near-stock geometry, so this pivot abandons the wedge entirely (2deg) and instead braces the airframe with genuine sweep triangulation (40/45 vs stock 31.4/36) plus a stretched 1.15 wheelbase for lower disk loading, growing plates 1.10/1.10 to keep tongue bolts anchored and testing whether geometric bracing plus moment-arm r

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Frogs

Think of the shapes of frogs when coming up with new ideas.

- A frog at rest is a low, wide squat: mass carried close to the ground
  plane, limbs folded tight against the body. What is the quadcopter
  equivalent of tucking in — minimal deck gap, battery laid flat, arms
  hugging the plate?
- A frog mid-leap is the opposite: limbs at full asymmetric extension,
  rear legs long and powerful, front legs short and braced for landing.
  Try strongly asymmetric sweep — long swept-back rear arms, short
  forward arms.
- Webbed feet spread load over area with almost no mass. Where can a
  wide, thin structure replace a thick one?
- Frog skin is compliant; frogs absorb landings through flex, not
  stiffness. Consider the flexible filaments with generous arm width
  instead of maximum-stiffness carbon.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 7 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 5e3f8986 — Abandon the all-carbon floor-mass tension-truss for frog-skin compliance: PETG with a wide, only lightly waisted arm and real thickness margin (well past the thickness/waist combo that killed every prior printed-material probe), on safe stock-adjacent geometry so only the material+cross-section axis changes.flew at 10.562 Wh/km
  • candidate 13a83031 — Frog mid-leap asymmetry: soften the failed 30.5/55 deg sweep split to 32/48 deg and grow plate_length/width_scale past the rule-of-thumb minimum (1.15/1.18 vs the ~1.12 needed) so the long swept rear tongue bolts stay anchored, reinvesting the abandoned battery-wedge budget into rear arm reinforcement instead.flew at 9.313 Wh/km
  • candidate 0522103c — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Abandon the wedge trick and standard stance entirely — tuck the frog in: flat battery (no wedge), short wheelbase, minimal deck_gap, and a wide-flat (unwaisted) low-mass arm that spreads load over area instead of relying on tilt or truss depth.
  • candidate 14498172 — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Abandon near-stock sweep in favor of a frog mid-leap: short braced front arms near their sweep floor and long swept-back rear arms with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule, letting asymmetric geometric bracing (not the maxed wedge) carry the airframe.
  • candidate e0fc3c12 — rejected pre-flight (rotor too close to deck/battery) — Frog-at-rest tucked posture: replace the failed short-wheelbase (0.8) wide-arm design's rotor-overlap with a stock-length wheelbase so the wide, nearly-unwaisted webbed-foot arm has clearance, while keeping deck_gap low and battery wedge at zero (flat, ground-hugging) to abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely.

✦ claude designer · generation 19 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182, g18 8.161 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 6 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in
- g18 `364861df` fitness 8.621 Wh/km-agg — was: The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather than
- g18 `44d60067` fitness 9.071 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting fla
- g18 `677ff932` fitness 9.350 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty once

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 6 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate cc389578 — Every elite shares one trait: frontal area cut via maxed battery wedge on near-stock geometry, so this pivot abandons the wedge entirely (2deg) and instead braces the airframe with genuine sweep triangulation (40/45 vs stock 31.4/36) plus a stretched 1.15 wheelbase for lower disk loading, growing plates 1.10/1.10 to keep tongue bolts anchored and testing whether geometric bracing plus moment-arm rflew at 8.657 Wh/km
  • candidate 930f0f43 — Every elite is carbon at arm-mass floor; PLA+ has never been properly tested (its one random trial failed from arm overstress, not tip deflection), so this design breaks the monoculture with PLA+ on stock-adjacent, low-risk geometry but gives the arm real cross-section (width 1.0, light waist 0.85, thickness 0.007) and plates at 0.85x (above the 0.8x printed-material floor) to finally survive wherflew at 9.544 Wh/km
  • candidate 99aabf80 — Past probes changed only one lever at a time (wheelbase alone, sweep alone, wedge alone, gap alone) and each underperformed in isolation; this design stacks all four moderately at once: a modest 1.08 wheelbase stretch, real-but-restrained sweep triangulation (37/42) with matching 1.05 plate growth, generous 0.037 deck_gap to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode, and a still-strongflew at 8.671 Wh/km

✦ claude designer · generation 18 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182, g17 8.182 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 5 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):
fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg, frame 83 g, cf_plate: genes={"arm_length_scale": 0.95, "arm_width_scale": 0.75, "arm_waist_scale": 0.55, "arm_thickness": 0.004, "front_sweep_deg": 33.0, "rear_sweep_deg": 37.5, "plate_length_scale": 1.0, "plate_width_scale": 1.0, "deck_gap": 0.03, "battery_wedge_deg": 14.5, "plate_thickness_scale": 0.72, "material": 0.0} scenarios={"calm_warm": 3.44, "cold_headwind": 6.41, "crosswind": 5.0, "gusty_light": 4.53, "hot_thin": 3.69, "storm": 6.51}
