YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the 
- g17 `0e54d60f` fitness 10.724 Wh/km-agg — was: Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-
- g17 `60382b2b` fitness 9.285 Wh/km-agg — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the print
- g17 `f9ea3427` fitness 8.792 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright in

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 5 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 677ff932 — Every elite is 100% carbon at floor arm mass with near-stock geometry — break the material monoculture with genuinely reinforced PET-CF (moderate width/waist/thickness matching the one successful PA12-CF template, thick decks well past the 0.8x printed-material floor) on the exact stock-adjacent geometry already proven valid, isolating whether a tougher material earns back its density penalty onceflew at 9.350 Wh/km
  • candidate 364861df — The 25+12+14 rotor/deck-clearance rejects in the failure histogram have kept every elite's deck_gap pinned at 0.03-0.032 and wedge below 14.5, so this design spends real standoff mass to open deck_gap to 0.040 and buys enough vertical margin to combine the never-tried pairing of a stretched 1.15 wheelbase (lower disk loading) with the true 15deg wedge ceiling, testing whether clearance rather thanflew at 8.621 Wh/km
  • candidate 44d60067 — Every elite's arm sits at floor width with a deep waist (a thin truss) or, when tried otherwise, a full-bulk compression wall that failed outright -- this design tests the untried middle path of a wide-flange arm (width 1.1, only lightly waisted at 0.65, thickness still at floor) carrying moderate front/rear sweep triangulation with plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb, letting flaflew at 9.071 Wh/km
  • candidate d3491e80 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every elite has driven arm_length_scale to a narrow 0.95-0.97 band without ever probing the unexplored short region toward the 0.75 floor; this design shrinks the wheelbase to 0.80 while keeping the earned battery-wedge trick and floor-mass arm untouched, isolating whether cutting overall structure size beats the elite's moment-arm compromise despite the disk-loading cost.
  • candidate 172c80c4 — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Every prior triangulation attempt widened both sweeps symmetrically and either crushed tongue bolts or added dome bulk; this design triangulates asymmetrically instead, keeping a narrow low-drag front sweep for headwind while swinging only the rear sweep to 54 deg for tail bracing, growing plates just enough (1.22/1.18) to keep bolts seated -- an unexplored front/rear split that lets geometry carr
  • candidate c65565d6 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — The maxed battery-wedge trick has never been tested paired with a shorter-than-elite wheelbase and zero compensating bulk; this design abandons battery tilt entirely and shrinks arm_length_scale to 0.88 with every other axis at its ephemeral floor, betting a genuinely smaller lighter airframe beats the wedge-dependent local optimum outright rather than refining it.

✦ claude designer · generation 17 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191, g15 8.182, g16 8.182 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 4 generation(s)

CURRENT ELITES (best first):

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g3 `8e9a6993` fitness 9.202 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing: hold the front sweep near stock but swing the rear sweep far out to triangulate the airframe geometrically, letting arm width/thickness and deck plate thickness fall since the load path is braced by angle rather than bulk.
- g3 `c842b896` fitness 9.344 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller's tension-is-cheap truss: stretch the arm well beyond the elite's wheelbase for lower disk interference, but keep it light by deep-waisting it into a truss rather than a plate, betting the longer moment arm pays for itself in cruise efficiency.
- g6 `3e4926e1` fitness 9.542 Wh/km-agg — was: Material-diversity probe: break out of the all-carbon monoculture with tougher PA12-CF, compensating its lower stiffness with a thicker, wider, less-waisted arm so storm-gust flex loads that killed earlier printed-material entries are absorbed rather than fought.
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.
- g16 `8306b049` fitness 10.254 Wh/km-agg — was: Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite s
- g16 `96878b1c` fitness 8.782 Wh/km-agg — was: Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 4 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate f9ea3427 — Abandon the maxed-battery-wedge frontal-area trick and the arm's own minimum-mass floor is kept, but this time strength comes from a genuinely full geodesic dome: both sweeps swung far past stock (42/50 vs 31.4/36) with plate scales grown to the rule-of-thumb 1.28/1.24 to keep tongue bolts anchored, and deck_gap opened to 0.038 m to starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright inflew at 8.792 Wh/km
  • candidate 60382b2b — Break the all-carbon monoculture properly this time: keep the elite's proven safe stock-adjacent geometry (33/37.5 sweep, 1.0/1.0 plates, 0.032 deck_gap) so only the material axis changes, but give PA12-CF real reinforcement -- moderate width and waist (0.95/0.85, not the carbon family's deep truss) and mid-range thickness (0.0065 m) plus plate_thickness_scale 0.95 -- enough margin above the printflew at 9.285 Wh/km
  • candidate 0e54d60f — Test the opposite structural philosophy from every elite's tension-truss-at-minimum-mass assumption: stretch the wheelbase to 1.25 for genuinely lower disk loading, but this time pair it with a real full-width compression wall (width 1.1, waist 1.0, thickness 0.006) instead of a deep-waisted truss, on stock-adjacent sweeps and modest wedge so the long-arm-plus-bulk trade is isolated and its Wh/km-flew at 10.724 Wh/km

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `bd035022` INVALID (arm tip deflection (pet_cf)) — was: Target cold_headwind and crosswind directly: long slender tension-optimized arms for calmer disk interference plus an aggressive battery wedge to shrink frontal area, in a mid-weight PET-CF composite.
- g3 `07659e37` fitness 9.398 Wh/km-agg — was: Same lightweight PA12-CF geometry as the failed design but with deck_gap opened to 0.032 m and battery_wedge trimmed to 10 deg so the tilted battery no longer intrudes on rotor clearance.
- g3 `8e9a6993` fitness 9.202 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing: hold the front sweep near stock but swing the rear sweep far out to triangulate the airframe geometrically, letting arm width/thickness and deck plate thickness fall since the load path is braced by angle rather than bulk.
- g3 `c842b896` fitness 9.344 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller's tension-is-cheap truss: stretch the arm well beyond the elite's wheelbase for lower disk interference, but keep it light by deep-waisting it into a truss rather than a plate, betting the longer moment arm pays for itself in cruise efficiency.
- g6 `3e4926e1` fitness 9.542 Wh/km-agg — was: Material-diversity probe: break out of the all-carbon monoculture with tougher PA12-CF, compensating its lower stiffness with a thicker, wider, less-waisted arm so storm-gust flex loads that killed earlier printed-material entries are absorbed rather than fought.
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha
- g15 `168c3f15` fitness 8.182 Wh/km-agg — was: Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.
- g15 `722bb4a8` fitness 8.364 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.
- g15 `9780185b` fitness 8.321 Wh/km-agg — was: Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 3 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 8306b049 — Every printed-material probe so far died to arm tip deflection because it kept the carbon family's deep-waist tension-truss trick on a weaker material; this design abandons that trick entirely -- full-width, unwaisted, near-max-thickness PA12-CF arm plus thick decks -- to finally test whether a toughness-first, non-carbon frame is even viable, breaking the all-carbon monoculture that every elite sflew at 10.254 Wh/km
  • candidate 96878b1c — Long-wheelbase and maxed-battery-wedge have each been tested in isolation (long arm alone underperformed at 9.08-9.14, wedge alone tops out the current elites at 8.18) but never combined -- this design stacks both frontal-area/disk-loading levers together with elite-floor arm bulk and only a modest plate-scale bump for the moderately swept tongues, staking out a genuinely unexplored corner of the flew at 8.782 Wh/km
  • candidate f52d65e3 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004, width ~0.75) and wheelbase has only ever been pushed longer (never shorter) despite props being fixed-size -- this pivot cuts the wheelbase itself toward its floor at safe stock sweeps, betting that 20% less arm material (a recurring tax in all six scenarios) beats the marginal disk-interference cost that longer-arm
  • candidate 70db0c9c — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Keep the short-wheelbase ephemeralization pivot (arm_length_scale 0.84, minimum arm bulk, maxed wedge, thin carbon deck) that the failed design was testing, but widen both sweeps to restore the rotor-tip clearance margin that a short radius with near-stock sweep destroyed, growing plate_length/width slightly so the tongue bolts stay seated under the larger sweep.

✦ claude designer · generation 15 · scheduled round · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417, g12 8.191, g13 8.191, g14 8.191 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 2 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `3aa66c71` INVALID (arm tip deflection (asa)) — was: Storm-hardened ASA workhorse: modest arm width and thickness plus a slightly grown plate scale give the gust-loaded worst-case scenario a stiffer, weather-tolerant airframe without ballooning mass.
- g0 `bd035022` INVALID (arm tip deflection (pet_cf)) — was: Target cold_headwind and crosswind directly: long slender tension-optimized arms for calmer disk interference plus an aggressive battery wedge to shrink frontal area, in a mid-weight PET-CF composite.
- g3 `07659e37` fitness 9.398 Wh/km-agg — was: Same lightweight PA12-CF geometry as the failed design but with deck_gap opened to 0.032 m and battery_wedge trimmed to 10 deg so the tilted battery no longer intrudes on rotor clearance.
- g3 `8e9a6993` fitness 9.202 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing: hold the front sweep near stock but swing the rear sweep far out to triangulate the airframe geometrically, letting arm width/thickness and deck plate thickness fall since the load path is braced by angle rather than bulk.
- g3 `c842b896` fitness 9.344 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller's tension-is-cheap truss: stretch the arm well beyond the elite's wheelbase for lower disk interference, but keep it light by deep-waisting it into a truss rather than a plate, betting the longer moment arm pays for itself in cruise efficiency.
- g6 `3e4926e1` fitness 9.542 Wh/km-agg — was: Material-diversity probe: break out of the all-carbon monoculture with tougher PA12-CF, compensating its lower stiffness with a thicker, wider, less-waisted arm so storm-gust flex loads that killed earlier printed-material entries are absorbed rather than fought.
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris
- g12 `30f271ae` fitness 8.363 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.
- g12 `3958e3b9` fitness 8.485 Wh/km-agg — was: Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to 
- g12 `3ae2747c` fitness 8.191 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather tha

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

Propose exactly 3 NEW genome vectors that are meaningfully DIFFERENT from the elites and from each other — design hypotheses, not small perturbations. Build on what the trajectory and your earlier proposals' outcomes show worked; avoid repeating what failed. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 722bb4a8 — Geodesic-braced long-wheelbase with the wedge trick abandoned entirely: combine a moderate wheelbase stretch (lower disk loading) with genuine sweep triangulation on both arms, reinvesting the freed frontal-area budget into deck_gap margin, to test whether pure geometric bracing can beat the wedge-dependent elite cluster -- a combination two prior probes tried separately but never together.flew at 8.364 Wh/km
  • candidate 168c3f15 — Retest the ephemeralized-deck floor below 0.75x but with plate_length/width restored to the champion's safe 1.0x, isolating the thickness axis instead of conflating it with a shrunk plate scale that crushed the FC-stack web.flew at 8.182 Wh/km
  • candidate 9780185b — Retry the rear-sweep geodesic brace but grow plate_length_scale further (1.08 to 1.15) so the arm tongue bolts stay seated on the enlarged main plate, trimming battery wedge slightly to keep the frontal-area budget balanced.flew at 8.321 Wh/km
  • candidate 936f5712 — rejected pre-flight (plate web too thin (plate_main 1.07 mm, stock 1.90 mm)) — Radical deck ephemeralization: on top of the proven best-elite arm/sweep/wedge geometry, push both plate footprint (0.95/0.95) and plate thickness (0.70, true floor) down simultaneously -- an untested combined floor-push that bets the near-stock sweeps keep the FC-stack web-crush constraint satisfied while shaving the last easy grams off the deck.
  • candidate fd6a3ccf — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Asymmetric rear-triangulated dymaxion probe: keep the proven front geometry, floor-mass arm, and maxed wedge intact, but swing only the rear sweep well past the elite cluster (with the plate-scale bump the rule of thumb demands) to brace the rear disk pair geometrically against storm/crosswind gust loads, isolating the asymmetric front/rear sweep axis the DeadCat platform already implies but no el

✦ claude designer · generation 12 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417, g11 8.417 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 5 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `3aa66c71` INVALID (arm tip deflection (asa)) — was: Storm-hardened ASA workhorse: modest arm width and thickness plus a slightly grown plate scale give the gust-loaded worst-case scenario a stiffer, weather-tolerant airframe without ballooning mass.
- g0 `bd035022` INVALID (arm tip deflection (pet_cf)) — was: Target cold_headwind and crosswind directly: long slender tension-optimized arms for calmer disk interference plus an aggressive battery wedge to shrink frontal area, in a mid-weight PET-CF composite.
- g3 `07659e37` fitness 9.398 Wh/km-agg — was: Same lightweight PA12-CF geometry as the failed design but with deck_gap opened to 0.032 m and battery_wedge trimmed to 10 deg so the tilted battery no longer intrudes on rotor clearance.
- g3 `8e9a6993` fitness 9.202 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing: hold the front sweep near stock but swing the rear sweep far out to triangulate the airframe geometrically, letting arm width/thickness and deck plate thickness fall since the load path is braced by angle rather than bulk.
- g3 `c842b896` fitness 9.344 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller's tension-is-cheap truss: stretch the arm well beyond the elite's wheelbase for lower disk interference, but keep it light by deep-waisting it into a truss rather than a plate, betting the longer moment arm pays for itself in cruise efficiency.
- g6 `3e4926e1` fitness 9.542 Wh/km-agg — was: Material-diversity probe: break out of the all-carbon monoculture with tougher PA12-CF, compensating its lower stiffness with a thicker, wider, less-waisted arm so storm-gust flex loads that killed earlier printed-material entries are absorbed rather than fought.
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long
- g11 `579452c0` fitness 8.791 Wh/km-agg — was: Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.
- g11 `79219798` fitness 9.081 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.
- g11 `f2ccb428` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second ris

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 5 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 3ae2747c — The elite family has held plate_thickness_scale in a tight 1.19-1.25 cluster despite never being pushed downward toward the 0.7 floor -- this pivot isolates that untested mass tax by dropping deck plates to 0.75x while keeping the proven near-stock geometry and maxed battery wedge unchanged, testing whether carbon's stiffness-to-weight ratio makes the elite's thick decks an unearned tax rather thaflew at 8.191 Wh/km
  • candidate 30f271ae — Every elite spends its whole frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge while keeping sweeps timidly close to stock -- this design retreats the wedge to 6deg and instead braces the airframe with real (but bolt-safe) sweep triangulation plus thin decks, betting that geometric strength substitutes for both the wedge trick and the deck-plate bulk simultaneously.flew at 8.363 Wh/km
  • candidate 3958e3b9 — Prior isolated long-wheelbase tests (arm_length_scale 1.28-1.30) underperformed but were never paired with thin decks, so this design stretches to a moderate 1.15 with a rear-sweep brace for tip stability and drops plate_thickness_scale to 0.75, combining the moment-arm restructuring and ephemeral-deck axes that earlier probes tested only separately, while keeping the proven wedge trick intact to flew at 8.485 Wh/km
  • candidate 9492210a — rejected pre-flight (plate web too thin (plate_main 1.07 mm, stock 1.90 mm)) — Every elite already sits at the arm's tension-truss floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and the battery wedge's frontal-area ceiling (~15deg) yet the deck plate has never been pushed below stock scale -- this design keeps arm and sweep stock-neutral but drives plate_length/width_scale down to the 0.95 web-crush floor and drops wedge to near-zero, abandoning the maxed-wedge/clearance trade that cau
  • candidate 31a18097 — rejected pre-flight (rotor disks overlap (tip clearance)) — Every attempted design (elite or pivot) clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.30, leaving the short end of arm_length_scale (0.75-0.85) completely unexplored -- this design pushes toward that floor at 0.78 so motors sit closer together for a smaller crosswind silhouette and shorter, lighter arms, keeping arm bulk at its tension-truss minimum since a short lever arm carries far less bending moment and needs
  • candidate 2f0f8d19 — rejected pre-flight (arm tongue bolts miss the main plate) — Prior geodesic-dome pivots swept the airframe wide but always left the battery wedge near its ceiling, stacking the same rotor/deck clearance risk that dominates the reject histogram onto the plate-web risk of a big sweep change -- this design commits fully to Fuller's dome logic (rear sweep pushed to 50deg, front to 40deg, plate scales grown per the tongue-bolt rule of thumb) while dropping wedge

✦ claude designer · generation 11 · pivot round — “take a step back” ask · claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-5

what claude was asked

You are the designer in an automated evolutionary study of 3D-printable quadcopter frames. The geometry is the real TBS Source One V6 7-inch DeadCat, morphed by a genome; the mission is 2 km north + 2 km south at 12 m/s through six adverse-weather scenarios; fitness = mean Wh/km across scenarios + 0.5 x worst scenario (LOWER IS BETTER). The platform (battery, 2806 motors, 7x4 props, electronics) is fixed; only the frame genome varies.

GENES (name: min..max — meaning):
- arm_length_scale: 0.75..1.35 — arm shaft stretch (wheelbase / disk loading)
- arm_width_scale: 0.75..1.4 — arm width at shaft ends (stiffness vs drag)
- arm_waist_scale: 0.55..1.3 — extra mid-shaft narrowing (drag vs stiffness)
- arm_thickness: 0.004..0.009 — arm plate thickness in meters
- front_sweep_deg: 30.0..52.0 — front arm azimuth from nose
- rear_sweep_deg: 34.0..62.0 — rear arm azimuth from tail
- plate_length_scale: 0.85..1.3 — deck plate stretch along flight axis
- plate_width_scale: 0.85..1.25 — deck plate stretch across
- deck_gap: 0.02..0.045 — standoff length in meters (stack space, prop-deck gap)
- battery_wedge_deg: 0.0..15.0 — battery tilt on the top plate (frontal area)
- plate_thickness_scale: 0.7..1.6 — x2 mm deck plates (mass vs stiffness)
- material: 0.0..0.999 — 0-0.17 carbon plate, 0.17-0.33 PA12-CF, 0.33-0.5 PET-CF, 0.5-0.67 PLA+, 0.67-0.83 PETG, 0.83-1 ASA

BEST-PER-GENERATION TRAJECTORY (Wh/km-agg): g0 8.976, g1 8.976, g2 8.976, g3 8.911, g4 8.849, g5 8.804, g6 8.417, g7 8.417, g8 8.417, g9 8.417, g10 8.417 — flat (no >=0.5% improvement) for the last 4 generation(s)

YOUR EARLIER PROPOSALS AND HOW THEY FARED:
- g0 `3aa66c71` INVALID (arm tip deflection (asa)) — was: Storm-hardened ASA workhorse: modest arm width and thickness plus a slightly grown plate scale give the gust-loaded worst-case scenario a stiffer, weather-tolerant airframe without ballooning mass.
- g0 `bd035022` INVALID (arm tip deflection (pet_cf)) — was: Target cold_headwind and crosswind directly: long slender tension-optimized arms for calmer disk interference plus an aggressive battery wedge to shrink frontal area, in a mid-weight PET-CF composite.
- g3 `07659e37` fitness 9.398 Wh/km-agg — was: Same lightweight PA12-CF geometry as the failed design but with deck_gap opened to 0.032 m and battery_wedge trimmed to 10 deg so the tilted battery no longer intrudes on rotor clearance.
- g3 `8e9a6993` fitness 9.202 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing: hold the front sweep near stock but swing the rear sweep far out to triangulate the airframe geometrically, letting arm width/thickness and deck plate thickness fall since the load path is braced by angle rather than bulk.
- g3 `c842b896` fitness 9.344 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller's tension-is-cheap truss: stretch the arm well beyond the elite's wheelbase for lower disk interference, but keep it light by deep-waisting it into a truss rather than a plate, betting the longer moment arm pays for itself in cruise efficiency.
- g6 `3e4926e1` fitness 9.542 Wh/km-agg — was: Material-diversity probe: break out of the all-carbon monoculture with tougher PA12-CF, compensating its lower stiffness with a thicker, wider, less-waisted arm so storm-gust flex loads that killed earlier printed-material entries are absorbed rather than fought.
- g6 `50612ef7` fitness 8.804 Wh/km-agg — was: Keep the elite's proven deep-waisted, near-stock-swept geometry but trade a little battery-wedge frontal-area gain for real vertical clearance: deck_gap opened to 0.033 m and wedge trimmed to 11 deg so the tilted battery and rotor plane stop colliding, the failure mode both rejected designs shared.
- g9 `2e045ca5` fitness 8.805 Wh/km-agg — was: Fuller tension-truss arm: stretch the wheelbase to 1.15 for lower disk interference in cruise, betting the longer moment arm's cheap-tension bracing (deep waist, minimum thickness) pays for itself despite the extra reach, with stock-adjacent sweeps keeping the plate scales modest.
- g9 `2e258492` fitness 8.675 Wh/km-agg — was: Geodesic-dome bracing v2: swing the rear sweep out to 48 deg (well beyond the earlier failed 50 deg attempt) but this time grow both plate scales to 1.20 (comfortably above the ~1.12 the rule-of-thumb demands), letting triangulated geometry carry load so the arm can go to its minimum width, waist, and thickness.
- g10 `0437c36d` fitness 8.865 Wh/km-agg — was: Every elite has already driven arm mass to its floor (waist 0.55, thickness 0.004) and battery tilt to its ceiling (~15deg) yet plateaued at 8.42 Wh/km-agg with only timid sweep deviation from stock — this design abandons bulk entirely and pushes both sweeps far past stock (42/50 vs stock ~31/36) into genuine geodesic triangulation, growing plates to 1.28/1.22 to keep the tongue bolts anchored and
- g10 `5c5784b9` INVALID (arm tip deflection (petg)) — was: Every elite is an all-carbon monoculture; this design tests ephemeralization on the material axis instead, letting tougher PETG carry storm-gust loads through generous plate thickness (1.35x, well past the printed-material floor) while the arm returns to a stock-adjacent, moderately-waisted profile rather than the earlier failed PA12-CF probe's bulky wide arm, aiming to survive where that one brok
- g10 `b46801ba` fitness 9.143 Wh/km-agg — was: The elite family spends its entire frontal-area budget on a maxed battery wedge that sits right against the dominant rotor-clearance failure mode (17 rejects in the histogram); this design abandons wedge tilt almost entirely (2deg) and instead cuts disk loading by stretching the wheelbase toward its 1.30 ceiling on a cheap deep-waisted tension-truss arm, with a touch more thickness than prior long

USER INSPIRATION -- the researcher asked you to draw on the ideas below when proposing designs. Translate them into the genome; do not ignore them:
# Buckminster Fuller — ephemeralization

"How much does your building weigh?" Ask it of every proposal. Fuller's
whole program was ephemeralization: doing more and more with less and
less until eventually you do everything with nothing.

- Weigh everything. The score is energy per kilometer and every gram
  is a recurring tax paid in all six scenarios. Before admiring any
  feature, ask what it weighs and what it earns.
- Tension is cheap, compression is heavy. A fat straight arm resists
  bending like a wall; ask instead where the loads actually flow.
  Deep waisting keeps material only where the bending moment lives —
  an arm can be a truss pretending to be a plate.
- The geodesic insight: strength from geometry, not from bulk. Sweep
  angles triangulate the airframe; a well-chosen pair of front/rear
  sweeps braces the disk like a dome's great circles. Find the
  geometry that lets thickness and width fall.
- Dymaxion: maximum performance per unit of input. Compute each
  elite's Wh/km per gram of frame; propose designs that maximize that
  ratio rather than minimizing either term alone.
- "You never change things by fighting the existing reality." The
  stock frame is the existing reality. Build the new model — the
  radically light one that makes heaviness obsolete — rather than
  filing grams off the old one.


SCENARIO NOTES: storm (8 m/s wind, severe gusts, rain) dominates worst-case; cold_headwind rewards low frontal drag; hot_thin (thin air) rewards low mass; crosswind rewards a small side profile.

THE SEARCH HAS PLATEAUED: the best-so-far has not improved significantly for 4 generation(s). This is a PIVOT round. Take a step back: reason about WHY the current elite family has stopped improving — what shared trait is this local optimum built on, and what does the per-scenario data say it costs? Then propose exactly 3 PIVOTAL designs that abandon that trait and stake out genuinely different regions of the genome space. Do NOT refine the elites — a pivot that lands near them is a wasted round. Respect the hard constraints implied by the failure histogram. Known couplings: the deck gap must exceed 0.023 m for the FC stack; arm tongues collide when both sweeps sit at their minimums with wide arms; the tongue BOLTS must stay on the main plate -- sweeps far from stock (front 31.4, rear 36.0) need plate_length_scale/plate_width_scale to grow with them (rule of thumb: keep sweeps within ~6 deg of stock unless you also raise the plate scales by ~0.1 per extra 5 deg); the FC-stack holes stay PINNED while the plates scale, so plate scales below ~0.95 crush the material webs between holes and cutouts (min 80% of the stock web is enforced); and printed materials (anything but cf_plate) need plate_thickness_scale >= 0.8 (>= 1.6 mm plates).

Respond with a JSON object of the shape:
{"proposals": [{"rationale": "<one sentence>", "genes": {"arm_length_scale": <float>, "arm_width_scale": <float>, "arm_waist_scale": <float>, "arm_thickness": <float>, "front_sweep_deg": <float>, "rear_sweep_deg": <float>, "plate_length_scale": <float>, "plate_width_scale": <float>, "deck_gap": <float>, "battery_wedge_deg": <float>, "plate_thickness_scale": <float>, "material": <float>}}, ...]}

what it proposed

  • candidate 579452c0 — Abandon the maxed-wedge frontal-area trick entirely (wedge -> ~1.5 deg) and instead brace the airframe geometrically with real (not timid) sweep triangulation, using the freed-up wedge budget to open deck_gap to 0.036 m and starve the dominant rotor/deck-clearance failure mode outright.flew at 8.791 Wh/km
  • candidate f2ccb428 — Break the all-carbon monoculture with PETG for storm-rain toughness, but unlike every prior failed printed-material probe, pair it with genuinely reinforced arm geometry (width 1.0, waist 0.85, thickness 0.0065) and thick plates (1.3x) so the tip-deflection failure that killed asa/pet_cf/petg/pa12_cf attempts can't recur, while stock-adjacent sweeps and a de-maxed wedge avoid stacking a second risinvalid — arm tip deflection (petg)
  • candidate 79219798 — Every elite clusters at wheelbase 0.9-1.04; push arm_length_scale to 1.28 (near its 1.35 ceiling, an unexplored region) to cut disk loading via moment-arm restructuring instead of plate/wedge bulk, keeping sweeps stock-adjacent and wedge moderate so the long-arm variable is isolated rather than conflated with other risky changes as in prior long-wheelbase attempts.flew at 9.081 Wh/km
